event: no medical history of cardiovascular disease
initial ECG showed reversed R-wave progression
endoscopic catheter drainage of pancreatic necrosis
ECG showed new-onset ST elevation in inferior leads
transthoracic echocardiography visualized reduced left ventricular ejection fraction (25%)
diagnosed with acute inferior myocardial infarction
coronary angiography revealed sub-occlusion of proximal right coronary artery (RCA)
administered dual antiplatelet agents (200 mg aspirin, 20 mg prasugrel)
IVUS showed hypoechoic plaque with deep ultrasound attenuation
thrombolysis in myocardial infarction grade 3 flow
repeat IVUS detected lobulated hypoechoic mobile masses (fresh thrombi)
aspirated small red thrombi using 7-F aspiration catheter
acquired AT deficiency due to septic DIC
completed PCI without subsequent thrombus formation
platelet count did not decrease after PCI
